finding.summary: The vocal track exhibits elevated low-frequency energy around 100 Hz relative to the 1 kHz midrange, with a 6 dB difference suggesting missing high-pass filtering or proximity effect.
finding.probable_cause: No high-pass filter or corrective EQ is present in the empty FX chain, allowing low-frequency rumble or proximity effect to dominate.
finding.confidence_reason: Only three spectrum points are provided, but the 6 dB difference between 100 Hz and 1 kHz is significant, and the very low silence fraction (0.05) confirms a reliable active measurement.

evidence_refs.description: 0.05 ensures minimal dead air, so levels are not diluted

proposal.reason: The available operations (track volume, pan, FX parameter) cannot apply a high-pass filter to correct the spectral imbalance. Adjusting track volume would risk altering the post-fader send level to the reverb, and pan is irrelevant to the frequency content. An EQ plugin would be needed but is outside the given operation set.
